requirements_met: TimeoutError → infrastructure error with store unavailable message
EmbedServiceError → infrastructure error with temporarily unavailable message
ValueError → client error with sanitized validation message
Client vs infrastructure error distinction (error_type field)
validation_results.test_output_summary: 11 passed in 0.54s - all exception types covered (TimeoutError, EmbedServiceError, EmbedTimeoutError, ValueError variants), no-crash guarantee verified, credential hygiene verified, async support verified, ServerContext seam test passed
validation_results.code_quality: Excellent - clean separation of concerns, comprehensive exception mapping, robust sanitization, proper type hints with Python 3.14 syntax, zero lint errors
edge_cases_covered: Credential stripping (DSN, URL with credentials, host:port)

criteria_verification.notes: File src/fleet_memory/mcp/degradation.py exists with ToolResult dataclass (is_error, error_type, message, value fields) and tool_safe decorator supporting both sync and async functions. Verified by independent evidence.

TimeoutError caught and mapped to ToolResult(is_error=True, error_type='infrastructure', message='The memory store is unavailable'). Decorator never re-raises. Verified by independent test test_store_timeout_returns_unavailable (passed).

EmbedServiceError and EmbedTimeoutError caught and mapped to ToolResult(is_error=True, error_type='infrastructure', message='The operation is temporarily unavailable'). Verified by independent tests test_embed_error_returns_temporarily_unavailable and test_embed_timeout_returns_temporarily_unavailable (both passed).

ValueError, NamespaceValidationError, and UnknownPayloadTypeError mapped to ToolResult(is_error=True, error_type='client', message=<sanitized validation message>). Error type 'client' distinguishes from 'infrastructure'. Verified by independent tests test_validation_error_returns_client_error, test_unknown_payload_type_returns_client_error, test_generic_value_error_returns_client_error (all passed).

Message sanitization via _sanitize_message() strips postgresql:// DSNs, http(s):// URLs with credentials, host:port patterns, and secret file paths. All error messages pass through sanitizer. Verified by independent test test_messages_have_no_credentials (passed).

Test file tests/unit/test_mcp_degradation.py contains 11 test cases covering all three exception classes (TimeoutError, EmbedServiceError, EmbedTimeoutError), validation errors (ValueError variants), no-crash guarantee (test_wrapper_never_reraises), credential hygiene, async support, and ServerContext seam. All 11 tests passed in independent run.

Both src/fleet_memory/mcp/degradation.py and tests/unit/test_mcp_degradation.py pass ruff check and ruff format --check with zero errors. Python 3.14 type parameter syntax applied, imports properly organized.

rationale: All seven acceptance criteria verified by independent evidence. Orchestrator's independent test run confirms 11/11 tests pass, covering all required exception types, no-crash guarantee, and credential hygiene. All quality gates pass (tests, coverage, arch review, plan audit). Honesty discrepancies are reporting hygiene issues (should_fix severity) regarding orchestrator-managed paths incorrectly listed in files_modified, not work fabrication. The implementation is complete, correct, and production-ready.
